images.original_text:
戚繼光拳經
唐豪編著
武藝叢書第一輯之二

persons.posture: Standing in a forward-facing stance with left foot forward, right foot back; left hand raised in guard position, right hand holding a sword pointed downward; body slightly crouched, weight distributed 60% on front leg, 40% on rear leg.
persons.tactic: Defensive parry with sword while preparing for counter-strike; typical of close-quarters martial engagement.

persons.posture: Riding horse, leaning forward with right arm extended holding spear; left leg braced against saddle, right leg bent; body angled slightly to the side for balance and thrusting motion.
persons.tactic: Lance thrust from mounted position; used to disrupt enemy formation or target vulnerable points in armor.

persons.posture: Kneeling on one knee, left hand gripping staff near base, right hand raised above head; torso twisted slightly to the right; weight centered over supporting knee.
persons.tactic: Staff strike from kneeling position to target low or mid-level targets; emphasizes stability and sudden upward thrust.

images.original_text:
戚繼光拳經
著 編 唐 豪
武藝叢書 一輯之二
（圖中人物：左側一人，右臂前伸，右腿微屈，重心偏右，左手握拳於腰際，右手前推，似作格擋或擊打之勢）

persons.posture: 左腿微屈，右腿支撑身体重心，身体略微侧转，面向右前方；左手握拳置于腰际，右手向前平伸推出，掌心向前，手臂微屈，呈格挡或击打预备姿态；头部微侧，目光注视前方，重心偏右，体现攻防转换中的动态平衡。
persons.tactic: 此为戚继光拳经中典型的攻防转换动作，右手前推可解为“推山掌”或“封手击面”，用于化解对方攻击并顺势反击，符合明代军旅武术强调实用与迅捷的特点。

    "original_text": "少林以武著聞，始於隋末。十三僧助太宗征王世充，十二僧事唐高祖，皆得封爵。唐高祖時，少林寺僧王仁則、薛萬徹、李孝恭等，皆為將軍。唐太宗時，少林寺僧少達、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、少林、

images.original_text:
（二）圖勢
懶扎衣出門架，子變下勢步單。
對敵若無膽向先空目眼明手便。
金雞獨立頸起裝腰橫拳相攙。
背隊牛隻倒還若叫苦連天。
威機光拳經

persons.posture: Standing in a dynamic martial stance with left leg forward and right leg back, body slightly crouched. Left arm is raised high above the head with palm facing outward, fingers extended. Right arm is bent at the elbow, hand held near the waist or hip area. Weight is distributed with more pressure on the front (left) leg, indicating readiness for movement or strike. The torso is angled slightly to the side, suggesting balance and focus toward the opponent’s direction.
persons.tactic: This posture appears to represent a defensive or preparatory stance in martial combat, possibly part of a sequence called 'Golden Chicken Standing Alone' (金雞獨立), which emphasizes stability and readiness. The raised hand may be used to deflect or control an incoming attack, while the lower hand prepares for a counter-strike or block.

persons.posture: In a wide-legged stance with feet spread apart, body turned sideways. Right arm is extended forward with palm open and fingers pointing upward, as if pushing or striking. Left arm is bent and held across the chest or shoulder level, possibly guarding or preparing for a follow-up motion. The head is turned toward the direction of the extended arm, indicating focus on the target. Weight is balanced between both legs, suggesting mobility and control.
persons.tactic: This figure demonstrates a forward striking or pushing technique, likely associated with the phrase 'Lazy Zha Yi' (懶扎衣) — a term referring to a relaxed yet alert posture used in combat. The extended arm suggests an offensive maneuver, possibly aiming to disrupt the opponent’s centerline or create distance. The stance allows for immediate transition into other techniques.

images.original_text: 探馬傳自太祖諸勢可降可變。進攻退閃弱生強，接應舉之至善。拗單鞭黃花聚追提腿左右難防。搶步上拳連劈沉香勢推倒太山。

persons.posture: Standing in a wide, grounded stance with feet shoulder-width apart. Left leg slightly bent, right leg extended back for balance. Arms are outstretched horizontally — left arm extended forward, right arm extended backward — palms facing down. Torso is slightly twisted to the left, weight distributed mostly on the left leg. Head turned toward the front, eyes focused forward.
persons.tactic: This posture represents a defensive or transitional movement in martial arts, likely part of a sequence involving footwork and hand positioning to evade or counter an opponent’s attack. The phrase “拗單鞭黃花聚追提腿左右難防” suggests a technique involving a twisting motion (拗) combined with a single whip-like strike (單鞭), followed by a feint or retreat (聚追) to confuse the opponent. The “搶步上拳連劈沉香勢推倒太山” indicates a rapid advance with consecutive punches and sweeping strikes, aiming to overwhelm the opponent’s defense.

persons.posture: In a dynamic, upward-reaching stance with right arm raised high above the head, palm open and facing upward. Left arm is bent at the elbow and held near the waist, possibly in a preparatory or blocking position. Legs are in a lunge or step-forward stance, with the right knee bent and left leg straightened behind. Body leans slightly forward, weight shifted onto the front leg. Head is tilted upward, gaze following the raised hand.
persons.tactic: This figure appears to be executing a striking or intercepting motion, possibly simulating a vertical strike or block using the raised arm. The accompanying text implies this is part of a coordinated offensive maneuver (“進攻退閃弱生強” — advancing when weak, retreating when strong), suggesting that the raised arm may serve as a feint or setup for a follow-up technique. The posture aligns with classical martial arts forms emphasizing fluid transitions between offense and defense.

images.original_text: 七步拳手足相顧，換步逼上下隨。龍君右手快腳如風，我自有護衛尊重。倒騎龍許伴，走訪進入逐我兵。衛怎伊力猛硬攻，怎當我連珠砲動。

persons.posture: Left figure in a low, forward-leaning stance with left foot forward and right foot back. Both hands are raised near the face in a defensive or preparatory block position. Body is angled slightly forward, weight distributed mostly on the front leg, suggesting readiness to advance or counterattack.
persons.tactic: Executing a defensive maneuver while preparing for an offensive strike; the posture suggests a transitional movement in close combat, possibly part of a sequence called 'Seven-Step Fist' as indicated by the text.

persons.posture: Right figure in a dynamic advancing stance with right foot forward and left foot trailing. Arms are extended forward in a striking or pushing motion, body leaning slightly forward with weight shifted onto the front leg. The head is turned toward the opponent, indicating focus on engagement.
persons.tactic: Initiating an aggressive attack or pressure point strike, consistent with the text describing 'Dragon Lord's hand moves swiftly like wind' — implying rapid, forceful strikes aimed at overwhelming the opponent’s defense.

images.original_text: 懸腳虛伸輕進，二換腿決不饒。輕起上一掌滿天星誰敢來比並。邱劉勢左攬右掌劈來腳入步進心。挪更拳法探馬，均打人一著命靈。

persons.posture: Standing in a low, stable stance with left foot forward and right foot back. Left arm raised high above head with palm facing outward; right arm bent at elbow, hand held near hip. Torso slightly twisted to the left, weight distributed mostly on front leg. Head turned toward the raised hand, eyes focused upward.
persons.tactic: Executing a high block or strike while preparing for a follow-up step. The movement is described as 'light step' and 'reaching for the sky star,' indicating an evasive or counter-attacking technique using upper body motion.

persons.posture: Balancing on left leg with right leg lifted high in a kicking or sweeping motion. Arms extended horizontally, palms facing forward, suggesting a wide defensive or striking posture. Body leans slightly backward for balance, weight centered over supporting leg. Head turned toward the direction of the kick.
persons.tactic: Performing a leg sweep or kick while maintaining balance and readiness to counterattack. The text references 'two leg changes' and 'no mercy,' implying a dynamic, aggressive transition between stances designed to unbalance an opponent.

images.original_text: 下插勢快，快得進步攙無別。鉤腳領臂不容離，上慈下取一跌。埋伏勢高弓待虎，圈套寸步難移。就機逢發應，他受打必定危。

persons.posture: Kneeling on left knee, right leg bent with foot flat on ground; left arm raised high with fist clenched, right arm extended forward at waist level; body slightly twisted to the right, weight distributed mostly on left leg, torso upright with head turned toward right.
persons.tactic: Executing a low-strike or trapping technique (‘下插勢’), preparing for a follow-up strike or takedown after disrupting opponent’s balance. The posture suggests readiness to exploit an opening created by the opponent’s forward movement.

persons.posture: Low squatting stance with both knees bent and feet wide apart; left arm extended forward with palm facing down, right arm bent at elbow with hand near shoulder; body leaning slightly forward, weight evenly distributed between legs, eyes focused ahead.
persons.tactic: Defensive or counter-attacking posture ('鉤腳領臂'), using leg hooking and arm control to unbalance opponent before delivering a low kick or sweep ('上慈下取一跌'). This is a classic close-range grappling or takedown setup.

images.original_text: 拋架子搶步披掛，上腿那怕他。識石橫左探快如飛，架一掌不知天地。抬肘勢防他弄腿，我截須忽高低。劈引推應要皆依初手腳忙急。

persons.posture: Stance: Wide horse stance with knees bent, body slightly crouched. Right arm extended forward in a pushing motion, left arm pulled back near hip. Head turned to right, eyes focused on opponent. Weight distributed evenly between both feet, center of gravity low for stability.
persons.tactic: Executing a defensive block and counterattack — the 'pushing hand' technique (推手) to intercept an incoming leg strike while preparing to strike with the palm or forearm. The posture suggests simultaneous defense and offense, emphasizing timing and redirection.

persons.posture: Stance: Forward advancing step with right foot forward, left leg trailing. Right arm extended forward in a striking or grabbing motion, left arm bent and raised near shoulder for balance or secondary attack. Torso slightly twisted, weight shifted forward onto the front leg. Eyes focused ahead toward opponent.
persons.tactic: Initiating an offensive maneuver — likely a leg sweep or strike (as referenced by '上腿那怕他'), combined with a hand strike to disrupt the opponent’s balance. The movement is dynamic and aggressive, aiming to exploit the opponent’s forward momentum.

images.original_text:
一步隨機應變，左右腿斷截連珠急勢固手風雷當我閃騰巧取。擠拿勢封腳套子左右壓一如四平直來逢我投怎快腿不得通融。
威繼光拳經
九

persons.posture: Stance: Low, forward-leaning martial stance with left leg bent and right leg extended back. Hands are extended forward in a defensive or intercepting gesture, palms facing outward. Body is angled slightly to the right, weight distributed forward on the front leg, suggesting readiness to advance or block.
persons.tactic: Executing a defensive maneuver to intercept an opponent’s advancing foot, using hand pressure to control or redirect the attack while maintaining balance and mobility.

persons.posture: Stance: Wide, grounded stance with legs apart, knees bent. One arm is extended forward in a blocking or striking motion, palm open and facing forward. The other arm is raised near the head, possibly for defense or balance. Torso is upright, weight evenly distributed between both legs, indicating stability and readiness to counterattack.
persons.tactic: Employing a simultaneous offensive and defensive posture — blocking an incoming strike while preparing to follow up with a counterattack, leveraging body alignment and timing to disrupt the opponent’s rhythm.

images.original_text:
中四平勢推固硬攻進陡腿難來。毽手逼他單手短打以熟為非。
伏虎勢側身垂腿但來接我前擲。
看姑子始不穩被一跌分明。

persons.posture: Kneeling in a low stance with one leg extended forward and the other bent behind. The upper body leans slightly forward, left arm raised high with fist clenched, right arm extended forward in a striking or blocking motion. Weight is distributed primarily on the front leg, with the back leg providing stability and power for forward movement.
persons.tactic: Executing a low, aggressive strike or block while maintaining balance and readiness to advance. This posture suggests a technique designed to disrupt an opponent’s center of gravity or initiate a close-range attack.

persons.posture: In a dynamic forward lunge with both feet planted wide apart. The torso is angled slightly backward, arms raised defensively with fists clenched near the face. The right leg is forward and bent, left leg trailing behind for balance. Body weight is shifted toward the front leg, preparing for either defense or counterattack.
persons.tactic: Defensive maneuvering while preparing to counterattack. The posture indicates readiness to absorb or deflect an incoming strike while positioning for a swift offensive response, possibly using the raised fists to guard or strike.

images.original_text:
威繼光拳經
雀地龍下擊腿法，前揭起後進紅拳。他退我雖顛補來短當休延。
朝陽手偏身防護無縫通透梁英。倒陣勢彈他一腳，好教俯仰也喪身。

persons.posture: Standing in a forward-facing stance with left leg slightly bent and right leg straight, body leaning slightly forward. Right arm raised high above head with palm facing outward; left arm extended downward with fist clenched near hip. Weight distributed mostly on right leg, torso upright with slight forward lean.
persons.tactic: Executing a '雀地龍下擊腿法' (Magpie Dragon Low Kick Technique), preparing to strike downward with the right leg while maintaining upper body control for defense or transition.

persons.posture: Lying on back in a sprawled position with legs bent and arms extended upward. Head tilted back, eyes looking up, body relaxed but in a defensive or fallen posture. Feet are off the ground, suggesting recent impact or evasion.
persons.tactic: Defensive maneuver after being struck or falling — possibly illustrating the consequence of the preceding attack, demonstrating how to recover or counter from a vulnerable position ('倒陣勢彈他一腳' — knock him down with a foot strike).

images.original_text:
雁翅身挨，快腿走不留停。追上穿庄一腿要加剪劈紅。
跨虎势那移脚，要腿去不使他知。左右跟掷一连施，失手剪刀分易。

persons.posture: Left figure in a dynamic forward lunge with right leg extended, left knee bent low. Right arm is raised high with palm open, left arm extended backward for balance. Body leans forward with weight shifted onto the front leg, suggesting an aggressive advancing motion.
persons.tactic: Executing a 'cross-leg' or 'cutting leg' technique (跨虎势), aiming to disrupt opponent’s stance by stepping forward with a powerful leg movement while simultaneously preparing for a striking or trapping action.

persons.posture: Right figure in a defensive crouch with both knees bent and body lowered. Arms are raised in a blocking or parrying posture — right hand near face, left hand extended outward. Feet are wide apart for stability, indicating readiness to evade or counterattack.
persons.tactic: Defensive maneuver using a low stance to absorb or deflect incoming attacks, possibly preparing to execute a 'shearing kick' (剪劈) as described in the text, which implies a rapid downward cutting motion targeting the opponent’s legs or torso.

images.original_text: 揚鶴肘出步顛烈攬下掌摘打其心。拳壓捉冤雄開手腳必須相應。當頭絕勢衝人怕進步虎直擂兩拳。他退閃我又順踹不跌倒他也忙然。

persons.posture: Standing in a forward-facing martial stance with left leg forward and right leg back. Left arm is raised high above head with palm open, fingers extended upward. Right arm is extended forward at chest level, palm facing outward. Body is slightly twisted to the right, weight distributed 60% on front leg, 40% on rear leg. Head turned slightly toward opponent, eyes focused forward.
persons.tactic: Executing a 'Yang He Zuo Chu Bu Dian Lie Lan Xia Zhang Zhai Da Qi Xin' technique — an offensive elbow strike combined with a downward palm grab targeting the opponent’s heart area, using body rotation and footwork to close distance while maintaining balance.

persons.posture: In a defensive posture with feet shoulder-width apart, knees bent. Both hands are raised near the face in a guard position, palms facing inward. Torso is slightly crouched and angled backward to evade attack. Weight is evenly distributed between both legs, ready to shift or retreat. Head is lowered slightly, eyes tracking the opponent’s movement.
persons.tactic: Defensive response to the incoming strike — employing 'Xuan Tou Jue Shi Chong Ren Pa Jin Bu Hu Zhi Lei Liang Quan' (head-turning, decisive counter-strike avoidance) by creating space and preparing for a counter-palm or kick after the attacker overcommits.

images.original_text: 順臂肘靠身攢，打滾快他難遮攔。復外絞刷回經掛一跌誰敢爭前。族鼓勢左右應，近他手橫劈雙行。絞靠跌人八識得虎抱頭要躲無門。

persons.posture: Stance: Wide horse stance with knees bent, body slightly crouched. Right arm raised high with fist clenched, left arm bent and pulled back near hip. Head turned right, eyes focused forward. Weight distributed evenly on both legs, center of gravity low for stability.
persons.tactic: Executing a close-range striking technique using the right fist to strike upward or forward while preparing to counterattack with the left hand. The posture suggests an offensive maneuver designed to disrupt opponent’s balance and create openings.

persons.posture: Stance: Similar wide horse stance, body angled slightly forward. Both arms are raised in defensive position — right hand near face, left hand extended outward. Feet planted firmly, weight shifted slightly forward to prepare for movement or counterstrike.
persons.tactic: Defensive posture with simultaneous readiness to counterattack. The positioning suggests blocking or parrying an incoming strike while preparing to launch a follow-up attack, possibly using a sweeping or chopping motion with the right hand.

persons.posture: Standing in a ready stance, left foot forward, right foot back; holding a bow drawn with left hand, right hand pulling string; body slightly crouched, eyes focused forward; weight distributed 60% on front leg, 40% on rear leg.
persons.tactic: Archery preparation — aiming at distant target, maintaining balance for rapid shot release.

persons.posture: Kneeling on one knee, right arm extended holding spear pointed downward; left hand gripping shaft near tip; torso upright, head turned slightly left; weight balanced over supporting knee and foot.
persons.tactic: Stabilized spear thrust — preparing to strike low or defend against advancing opponent.

persons.posture: Standing upright, facing right, holding staff vertically with both hands at chest level; feet shoulder-width apart, knees slightly bent; body aligned straight, gaze fixed ahead.
persons.tactic: Staff defense posture — ready to parry or initiate linear strikes in close combat.

persons.posture: Walking forward with left leg stepping ahead, right arm raised holding sword above shoulder; left arm bent across chest; body leaning slightly forward, weight shifting to lead leg.
persons.tactic: Sword advance — initiating offensive movement toward opponent with blade ready to slash.

persons.posture: Standing sideways, left arm extended outward palm up, right arm bent at elbow holding halberd horizontally; feet staggered, hips rotated; weight centered over both legs.
persons.tactic: Defensive gesture with halberd — warding off incoming attack while maintaining mobility.

persons.posture: Riding horse, right hand gripping reins, left hand holding shield raised high; body leaning forward, legs braced against saddle; eyes scanning battlefield.
persons.tactic: Mounted shield defense — protecting rider while advancing or retreating under fire.

persons.posture: On horseback, right arm swinging spear forward in lunge motion; left arm gripping mane for balance; legs tensed, torso twisted for momentum; weight transferred to front leg.
persons.tactic: Cavalry spear charge — delivering powerful thrust while maintaining control of mount.

persons.posture: Kneeling on ground, right hand holding bow, left hand nocking arrow; head tilted upward, eyes tracking target; body low to minimize exposure.
persons.tactic: Sniper positioning — taking cover while preparing to shoot at elevated or distant enemy.
